synopsis: A guilt-ridden mountaineer aboard a research vessel encounters an alien spacecraft that raises a colossal mountain of water from the Pacific Ocean. The story contrasts the climber's search for redemption with the story of aliens whose entire civilization evolved inside a hollow planet, giving them a radically inverted understanding of matter and gravity.

Written by engineers who designed many of the tools it covers, this book explains how to use MySQL replication, clustering, and monitoring features to build systems that withstand server failures and outages. It covers the binary log, MySQL Replicant Library, read-load scaling, and tools including MySQL Enterprise Monitor and GTIDs.

Charles Wheelan strips away technical formulas to explain the intuition underlying statistical analysis, showing how data and well-chosen tools can answer meaningful questions about the world. The book covers probability, inference, regression, and common statistical pitfalls using accessible examples and plain language.

sources.title: Naked Statistics - W. W. Norton & Company

synopsis: Set in 1930s Chicago, the novel follows Bigger Thomas, a young Black man trapped by poverty and racial subjugation on the South Side, whose accidental killing of his white employer's daughter sets off a fatal chain of events. Wright uses the ensuing murder investigation and trial to examine how systemic racism and economic oppression shape individual choices and moral culpability.

This practical guide teaches developers and data scientists how to apply deep learning methods to natural language processing tasks using the PyTorch library. It covers computational graphs, word embeddings, sequence modeling, and sequence-to-sequence architectures, with code examples throughout.

sources.title: Natural Language Processing with PyTorch - O'Reilly

synopsis: This hands-on guide, written by Hugging Face engineers, shows how to build and fine-tune transformer models for NLP tasks including text classification, named entity recognition, and question answering. It also covers cross-lingual transfer learning, training on limited labeled data, and making models production-ready through distillation, pruning, and quantization.

sources.title: Natural Language Processing with Transformers - O'Reilly

synopsis: This comprehensive reference explains cryptographic concepts and network security protocols, covering AES, IPsec, SSL, PKI standards, and web security in intuitive rather than purely mathematical terms. It also addresses the practical constraints and design trade-offs behind real protocol standards, including how implementation choices can introduce vulnerabilities even when underlying cryptography is sound.

sources.title: Network Security: Private Communications in a Public World - Pearson

synopsis: Case is a burned-out hacker whose nervous system has been sabotaged, cutting him off from cyberspace, until a mysterious employer recruits him for a high-stakes run against a powerful artificial intelligence. Gibson's debut novel defines the cyberpunk genre, imagining a near-future world of corporate arcologies, mercenary street operatives, and a sprawling virtual matrix called the matrix.

Former FBI lead hostage negotiator Chris Voss adapts high-stakes crisis negotiation techniques for everyday professional and personal situations. The book presents principles such as tactical empathy, calibrated questioning, and mirroring as tools for reaching better outcomes than conventional compromise-based approaches allow.

sources.title: Never Split the Difference - Porchlight Book Company

synopsis: Harari traces the history of human information networks from prehistoric oral traditions through scripture, witch trials, totalitarian bureaucracies, and contemporary social media, arguing that societies build cooperative systems through shared fictions rather than objective truths. The book examines how AI differs from all previous information technologies and poses urgent questions about who will control the networks shaping humanity's future.

sources.title: Nexus - Yuval Noah Harari official site

synopsis: In a near-future world, an experimental nano-drug called Nexus allows human minds to link together, and a young scientist caught enhancing the drug is forced into a covert government operation that draws him into international espionage across Asia and the US. The novel explores questions of cognitive liberty, government control, and the unpredictable consequences of technology that dissolves the boundary between individual minds.

Rose George sails aboard container ships and patrols with anti-piracy naval forces to illuminate the vast, largely invisible global shipping industry that moves ninety percent of world trade. The book examines the economics, labor conditions, environmental impact, and geopolitical tensions of an industry most consumers never think about.

sources.title: Ninety Percent of Everything - Macmillan Publishers

synopsis: Netflix co-founder Reed Hastings and management scholar Erin Meyer describe the unconventional principles behind Netflix's corporate culture, including radical transparency, high talent density, and replacing rules and processes with context and judgment. The book draws on interviews with current and former employees across the globe to explain how these practices evolved and why Hastings believes they drive sustained innovation.

Robert Wright argues that both biological evolution and human cultural history are shaped by a directional logic of non-zero-sum interaction, in which organisms and societies grow more complex by finding ways to cooperate rather than merely compete. Ranging from bacteria to the World Trade Organization, the book contends that this tendency toward increasing complexity and interdependence gives human history a meaningful trajectory.

sources.title: Nonzero: The Logic of Human Destiny - Wikipedia

synopsis: Pramod Sadalage and Martin Fowler explain how NoSQL databases depart from the relational model and when they offer advantages, covering key-value, document, column-family, and graph data stores through concrete examples using Riak, MongoDB, Cassandra, and Neo4j. The book introduces the concept of polyglot persistence, where architects select different data storage technologies to match different access patterns within the same application.

Christopher Alexander presents a theory of design as an adaptive process in which form must be shaped in response to the context of human needs, arguing that successful design proceeds by decomposing problems into semi-independent subsystems rather than addressing everything at once. The book introduces graph-theoretic methods for analyzing the structure of design problems and generating form that correctly fits its context.

sources.title: Notes on the Synthesis of Form - Wikipedia

synopsis: A young Chilean exile travels to East Germany after the 1973 coup and falls in love with the daughter of Cuba's ambassador to Moscow, leading to a marriage celebrated in Havana and a gradual, disillusioning encounter with the realities of revolutionary Cuba. The autobiographical novel traces how political idealism collides with authoritarian practice across the socialist world of the 1970s.

Investigative journalist Zeke Faux spent two years tracking the cryptocurrency boom, traveling from Bahamas resort compounds to a Cambodia trafficking operation powered by crypto fraud to document how a $3 trillion industry was built on hype and deception. The book centers on the 2022 collapse of FTX and the downfall of Sam Bankman-Fried, whom Faux followed from the height of his influence to his arrest.

sources.title: Number Go Up - Penguin Random House

synopsis: Andrew Kilpatrick presents a comprehensive biography of Warren Buffett, covering his investment philosophy, business decisions, and the growth of Berkshire Hathaway through detailed quarter-by-quarter performance records. The book draws on a wide range of sources to chronicle how Buffett built one of the largest fortunes in American history through disciplined value investing.
